ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Barbara and Antonina.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: On receiving your epistles, I was in all manner of ways delighted to hear of your well being, and I entreat Almighty God that He would guard you by His protection from malignant spirits in thought, and from perverse men, and from all contrariety; and that He would, with the grace of His fear, settle you in unions worthy of you, and cause us all to rejoice in your settlement.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But do you, most sweet daughters, rest your hope on His help, and, being always under the shadow of His defence, both by praying and by well doing, escape the plots of bad men.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For, whatever human comforts or adversities there may be, there are none, unless either His grace protects or His displeasure troubles you.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Rest therefore your hope on no one among men, but bind your whole soul to trust in Almighty God.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: While we sleep, then, He will protect you, of whom it is written, Behold he that keepeth Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep (Ps. cxx.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: As to your saying that you are in haste to approach the threshhold of the blessed Peter, Prince of the apostles, I wish exceedingly, and wait with fervent desire, to see you in his church united to husbands well worthy of you; that so both you may obtain some little comfort from me, and I no little joy from your presence.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I have also commended your causes to thy most reverend brother the bishop John, and to Romanus the guardian (defensori), that under God they may accomplish what they have begun.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Your present of two racanæ, which you sent me word were your work, I accepted gladly.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But yet know ye that I did not believe the word you sent me.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For you are seeking praise from the work of others, seeing that you have perhaps never yet put hand to spindle.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Nor yet does this circumstance distress me, since I wish you to love the reading of Holy Scripture, that, so long as Almighty God shall unite you to husbands, you may know how you should live and how you should manage your houses.
